footnotes.text: Jesus does not abrogate the Sabbath Law, but He teaches that it should be interpreted in a reasonable way.

The prohibition against making known His miracles, usually to be accounted for by His desire to avoid the over-excitement which kept people from giving proper attention to His preaching, was probably due in this case to the wish of avoiding conflict with the Pharisees.
The victory of Jesus over the demons indicated that He was the Messias. The king was already gathering His people.
The sin against the Holy Spirit is to ascribe to the devil the works of the Holy Spirit. One who thus attacks directly the source of all grace, rejects the source of salvation.
An idle word is one which profits neither the speaker nor the hearer. If the word is merely useless, its utterance is not seriously wrong.
Jesus refuses a sign asked for by the incredulous, to be given under conditions fixed by themselves. He will, however, when the time has come, give them the sign of Jonas, that is, the Resurrection.
